title: Fixing Git Commit Hash Validation for Long Hashes

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates a given Git commit hash. The commit hash should be 40 characters long and consist of hexadecimal digits. If the hash is valid, return `true`; otherwise, return `false`. Note that commit hashes can be very long, but in this case, we only care about the first 40 characters. You should also validate that the hash consists of only hexadecimal digits.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Git commit hash
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the commit hash is valid
statement.notes: Time complexity O(n) where n is the length of the hash

constraints.description: The length of the commit hash

examples.explanation: The given hash is 40 characters long and consists of hexadecimal digits
The given hash is not 40 characters long
The given hash does not consist of hexadecimal digits

hints.text: Check if the length of the hash is 40
Check if the hash consists of hexadecimal digits

editorial.title: Validating Git Commit Hashes
editorial.intuition: To validate a Git commit hash, we need to check if it's 40 characters long and consists of hexadecimal digits.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a brute-force approach by iterating over the hash and checking each character.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a more efficient approach by using a regular expression to match hexadecimal digits.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a regular expression to match hexadecimal digits and check if the length of the hash is 40.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the regular expression will match hexadecimal digits and the length check will ensure the hash is 40 characters long.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: 1. Check if the length of the hash is 40. 2. Use a regular expression to match hexadecimal digits.
